questions.statement: Which reproductive part(s) is/are essential for fertilisation?
Which of the following is correct about fertilisation in plants?&nbsp;
options: The male germ-cell produced by the ovule fuses with the female gamete present in the pollen tube.&nbsp; /
The male germ-cell produced by pollen grain fuses with the female gamete present in the stigma. /
The male germ-cell produced by pollen grain fuses with the female gamete present in the ovule.&nbsp; /
The male germ-cell produced by the ovary fuses with the female gamete present in the ovule. /

questions.statement: When we are standing in a bus facing towards the driver and the bus begins to move straight suddenly, we tend to fall:
In which of the following life processes is the creation of a DNA copy, a basic event?
All of the following are examples of connective tissue, EXCEPT:
In which of the following organisms can individuals change sex, indicating that sex is NOT genetically determined?
Doubly-charged helium ions are also known as which of the following?
<!-- x-tinymce/html -->In a common salt/sodium chloride (NaCl), ions of which element is positively charged and negatively charged, respectively?
options: Ions of both Na and Cl are positively charged. /
Ions of both Na and Cl are negatively charged. /
Na ions are positively charged, and Cl ions are negatively charged.&nbsp; /
Cl ions are positively charged, and Na ions are negatively charged. /
